concerns.text: Constitution Principle VI (Neuroimaging Data Provenance) requires raw fMRI data to be obtained 'exclusively through the HCP API'. The plan and research.md explicitly define a fallback to 'verified static URLs' (parquet files) if API access is restricted. This fallback violates the 'exclusively' constraint of Principle VI, creating a fundamental conflict between the plan's robustness strategy and the constitutional data provenance rule.

Constitution Principle VII (Statistical Correlation Integrity) mandates logging the 'correlation-coefficient threshold (> 0.3)'. The plan, spec, and data model define the analysis and reporting of r-values but do not mention implementing or logging a specific threshold of 0.3 as a gating or logging parameter. The plan fails to exercise this specific constitutional requirement.

The plan lists 'global efficiency' in the 'Scale/Scope' and 'Technical Context' sections as a metric to be computed, but the 'Constitution Check' and 'Summary' sections only list 'modularity, participation coefficient, within-module degree'. The data-model.md defines 'ConnectivityMatrix' attributes but omits 'global efficiency' entirely, despite it being listed in the spec (FR-003) and plan context. This creates an inconsistency in the defined entity attributes across documents.
